anchor_results.name: 2008 GFC trough
anchor_results.brief: Lehman+ deep recession; oil bust drives YoY CPI to ~-2%

anchor_results.name: 2010-12 expansion
anchor_results.brief: Slow but steady job growth (PAYEMS YoY +1.5-2%), CPI ~2-3%

anchor_results.name: 2015-16 oil bust
anchor_results.brief: Mfg recession, CPI YoY near zero, oil bust deflation

anchor_results.name: 2018 Q4 vol shock
anchor_results.brief: Growth still strong YoY (PAYEMS +1.7%); market stress

anchor_results.name: 2019 H2 slowdown
anchor_results.brief: Mixed: services strong, mfg recession; CPI ~1.8%

anchor_results.name: 2020 COVID recession
anchor_results.brief: Catastrophic growth shock; CPI YoY collapse but not deflation

anchor_results.name: 2020H2 catch-up
anchor_results.brief: Recovering but YoY still deep negative; CPI YoY ~1.4%

anchor_results.name: 2022 H1 inflation
anchor_results.brief: PAYEMS +5-6% YoY catch-up, CPI peaks at 9.1% in June

anchor_results.name: 2023 high inflation
anchor_results.brief: Soft landing but CPI still 3-4%, payrolls +2.5%

anchor_results.name: 2025 tariff shock
anchor_results.brief: Breakevens spike; equity drawdown; growth signals mixed
